mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction when UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing and utility output, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 210.0, persistent inflationary pressure forces markets to price in prolonged monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 210.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption sectors, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when GDPC1 and RSAFS both grow, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when INDPRO drops below the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during slowdown regimes.
